families.rationale: ActivityPlan descriptor codec is a pure durable boundary for the closed plan algebra.
GraphDiff to ActivityPlan compilation is pure graph interpretation.
Compensation planning maps operations and durable completion order to pure inverse/recovery meaning.
Execution scenario expectations are retained only as closed catalogue/partial-order data; actual runner/coordinator behavior remains outside #739.
Planning scenario catalogues are deterministic graph/plan expectation data.
Recovery planning constructs fresh canonical graph transition plans without durable recovery service behavior.
Saga journal projection is a pure interpreter over canonical activity events.
Saga syntax is immutable callback-free data.
Saga state is reconstructed from closed events without stores or effects.
Scheduling is a pure interpreter from ActivityPlan and SagaState to ready/running/waiting/blocked views.
Scheduling scenario evidence is deterministic pure schedule catalogue data.

laws: Every #739 retained family must have no remaining live required-core inventory entries after closeout.
ActivityPlan remains graph-interpretation output, not a user-authored arbitrary workflow language.
Saga, scheduler, compensation, and recovery remain pure data/interpreters.
Aggregate suite passes do not replace per-family successor evidence.
No operation/runtime store behavior is copied into pure core to make tests pass.
